title: Transition from ePost Connect to LiquidFiles for the submission of Natural Health Product site licence applications - Canada.ca
content:
## Transition from ePost Connect to LiquidFiles for the submission of Natural Health Product site licence applications
The  Natural Health Products Site Licensing and Good Manufacturing Practices  Inspection Bulletin No. 11, March 30, 2026 (from the Health Product Compliance Directorate)
- Transition       from ePost Connect to LiquidFiles
- How to submit an SL submission via LiquidFiles
- Corresponding on your submission via       LiquidFiles
- Requesting a company code (for new companies)
- Trading Partner Agreements no longer required

## Transition from ePost Connect to LiquidFiles
To improve our processes and address  known and on-going issues with the ePost Connect platform, Health Canada will  be transitioning to a new platform: LiquidFiles.
As of March 30, 2026, Health  Canada will begin using LiquidFiles to:
- receive all site licence ( SL ) submissions, including  new applications, amendments, notifications, relinquishments and renewals
- send and receive all submission-related  correspondence, including clarimails and information request notices ( IRN )
- issue regulatory decisions for submissions,  including site licences, issuance letters and notices of refusal
There will be a one month  transition period from March 30, 2026 to April 30, 2026. During this time, SL  submissions will be accepted through ePost Connect or LiquidFiles.
As of May 1, 2026,  Health  Canada will stop accepting any site licence submissions and new correspondence in  ePost Connect. Only SL submissions received in LiquidFiles will be  processed. We will reject submissions sent through other means.
Important note: If you have an open  Clarimail, IRN or Product Information Form ( PIF ) that was sent to you through  ePost Connect, please respond to that request in ePost Connect as instructed in  that document. Following your response, any additional requests will be sent to  you via LiquidFiles.
This  change in process only applies to site licensing. For NHP product licence  applications, continue to follow the process described in Electronically contacting the Natural and Non-prescription Health  Products Directorate .

## How to submit a SL submission via LiquidFiles
All SL submissions must be  submitted here:
- Site Licence Submission Gateway:  LiquidFiles
You do not need a LiquidFiles  account to use or access the Gateway . To access the Gateway, you will go  through a simple email authentication step for security purposes. You will be asked to “Login” each time you  use the portal. Click on the ‘Authenticate’ button and a login token will be  sent to your email.
To avoid delays with processing, when  you submit information in LiquidFiles:
- Use the following format for your subject line: [Company Code] – [Company Name] – SL [ Submission  Type]
- [Company Code] – [Company Name] – SL [ Submission  Type]
- For your subject line, choose from one of the  following submission types: New application Amendment Renewal Notification Relinquishment
- Submission packages must be less than 10 GB . The Gateway accepts compressed files (that is,  zip file folders).
- The Gateway accepts compressed files (that is,  zip file folders).

## Corresponding on your submission via LiquidFiles
When you receive a secure message  from Health Canada in LiquidFiles, you will be notified by email. The email will  be from the following email address:
hpcd.nhp.sl-dcps.psn.le@hc-sc.gc.ca <liquidfiles.support-soutien.liquidfiles@hc-sc.gc.ca>
The email will include buttons to  ‘Download Files’ or to ‘Reply to this Secure Message’. When you click either of these buttons, LiquidFiles  will require you to authenticate your email for security purposes. You do  not need a LiquidFiles account to download or reply to the secure message .

## Requesting a company code (for new companies)
If you are a new company applying  for a site licence, you will need a company code before you can submit a  submission in LiquidFiles. Request a company code by emailing hpcd.nhp.sl-dcps.psn.le@hc-sc.gc.ca .
- Use the subject line: “New Company Registration”
- In the body of the email, include: company name company address senior official name, telephone number and email  address
- senior official name, telephone number and email  address
If you already hold or held a site  licence, you can find your company code on the following documents:
- Information Request Notices ( IRNs )

## Trading Partner Agreements no longer required for Site Licence applications
As of March 30, 2026, you no  longer need to apply for a Trading Partner Agreement before applying for a new  site licence.
This does not apply to NHP  product licences . You must still apply for a Trading Partner Agreement to  submit product licence  applications to the Natural and Non-prescription Health Products  Directorate ( NNHPD ).
If you have questions, email the Natural  Health Products Licensing and Inspection Division of the Regulatory Operations  and Enforcement Branch at hpcd.nhp.sl-dcps.psn.le@hc-sc.gc.ca .
